name: research-atlas
description: Given a research topic or question, searches real academic sources (arXiv, Crossref, OpenAlex) and returns an organized, trustworthy reading list — grouped by theme, flagged for conflicting findings, and honest about what it could not find or verify. Cites only papers it actually retrieved.

skills.name: Literature search & reading list
skills.description: Search academic sources for a research topic or question and return a structured reading list grouped by theme, with citation counts and DOIs/links, conflicting findings flagged, and explicit notes on gaps or unverifiable claims. Cites only papers actually retrieved from arXiv, Crossref, or OpenAlex.
examples: Find recent work on graph neural networks for drug discovery.
I'm starting a lit review on social determinants of health — give me an organized reading list.
What does the literature say about CRISPR off-target effects since 2021?
Summarize key papers on transformer interpretability, grouped by approach.
